definition_en: (of a bomb) to explode; (of a gun) to be fired

examples.text: A bomb went off in the city centre.

definition_en: (of food or drink) to go bad

examples.text: This milk has gone off. It smells terrible.

definition_en: to lift something or someone up from a surface

examples.text: She picked up the phone and dialled.

definition_en: to collect someone, typically in a car

examples.text: I'll pick you up at the station at six.

definition_en: to learn a skill or language informally

examples.text: She picked up some Spanish while living in Madrid.

definition_en: to arrive, especially unexpectedly or late

examples.text: She turned up at the party without an invitation.

definition_en: to increase the volume or heat

examples.text: Could you turn the radio up? I can't hear it.

definition_en: to have a good relationship with someone

examples.text: Do you get on well with your colleagues?

definition_en: to board a bus, train, plane, etc.

examples.text: We got on the bus at Victoria Station.

definition_en: to stop fighting or arguing and accept that you cannot win

examples.text: She gave in and let the children stay up late.

definition_en: to stop doing something you do regularly

examples.text: I gave up smoking three years ago.

definition_en: to leave a bus, train, plane, etc.

examples.text: I got off the bus at the wrong stop.

definition_en: to search for information in a book or on the internet

examples.text: I looked the word up in the dictionary.

definition_en: to investigate or examine the facts

examples.text: The police are looking into the matter.

definition_en: to take care of someone or something

examples.text: Could you look after the children while I go out?

definition_en: to feel pleased and excited about something going to happen

examples.text: I'm really looking forward to the holidays.

definition_en: (of a plane) to leave the ground

examples.text: The plane took off on time.

definition_en: to remove a piece of clothing

examples.text: He took off his jacket and sat down.

definition_en: to put a piece of clothing on your body

examples.text: She put on her coat and went out.

definition_en: to reduce the volume or heat

examples.text: Could you turn the music down? It's too loud.

definition_en: to reject an offer or request

examples.text: I turned down the job offer because the salary was too low.

definition_en: to enter a room or building

examples.text: Come in and sit down.

definition_en: to leave the house to do something enjoyable

examples.text: We went out for dinner last night.

definition_en: to eat at a restaurant instead of at home

examples.text: We usually eat out on Friday evenings.

definition_en: to move from standing to sitting position

examples.text: Please sit down and make yourself comfortable.

definition_en: to move from sitting to standing position

examples.text: Everyone stood up when the judge came in.

definition_en: to find the answer by thinking or calculating

examples.text: I can't work out how to use this phone.

definition_en: to do physical exercise

examples.text: I try to work out at the gym three times a week.

definition_en: to delay doing something, to postpone

examples.text: The meeting has been put off until next week.

definition_en: to make someone not want to do something

examples.text: The bad weather put us off going to the beach.

definition_en: to continue doing something

examples.text: Carry on with your work while I make a phone call.

definition_en: to think of an idea, plan, or solution

examples.text: She came up with a brilliant idea.

definition_en: to mention a subject or start to talk about it

examples.text: She brought up the subject of money.

definition_en: to look after a child until they are an adult

examples.text: She was brought up by her grandparents.

definition_en: to discover a fact or piece of information

examples.text: I need to find out what time the train leaves.

definition_en: to do or complete something planned or ordered

examples.text: The police carried out a thorough investigation.

definition_en: to tell someone something they did not already know

examples.text: He pointed out the mistakes in my essay.

definition_en: to deal with a problem or organise something

examples.text: We need to sort out this problem before it gets worse.

definition_en: to take control of something (company, responsibility)

examples.text: A large company took over the business.

definition_en: to consider something carefully before making a decision

examples.text: I need some time to think it over.

definition_en: to recover from an illness or difficult experience

examples.text: It took him a long time to get over his divorce.

definition_en: to give someone back money you owe them

examples.text: I'll pay you back on Friday.

definition_en: to return something to the person who gave it to you

examples.text: I must give the books back to the library.

definition_en: to use all of something so there is none left

examples.text: We've run out of milk. Can you buy some?

definition_en: to escape from a person or place

examples.text: The thief got away in a stolen car.

definition_en: to get rid of something you no longer need

examples.text: I threw away all my old school books.

definition_en: to give something to someone without asking for payment

examples.text: She gave all her old clothes away to charity.

definition_en: to walk around a place to see what is there

examples.text: We spent an hour looking round the museum.

definition_en: to guide someone around a new place

examples.text: Let me show you around the office.

definition_en: to think that you are better than someone else

examples.text: He looks down on people who didn't go to university.

definition_en: to admire and respect someone

examples.text: She really looks up to her older sister.

definition_en: to disappoint someone by failing to meet expectations

examples.text: I'm counting on you — don't let me down.

definition_en: to have an argument and stop being friendly

examples.text: She's fallen out with her best friend.

definition_en: to start a company, organisation, or system

examples.text: She set up her own business three years ago.

definition_en: to cancel something that has been planned

examples.text: They called off the wedding at the last minute.

definition_en: to understand or solve something by thinking

examples.text: I can't figure out how to open this door.

definition_en: to take action to solve a problem or handle a situation

examples.text: How do you deal with stress at work?

definition_en: to explain the reason for something; to form a proportion

examples.text: Exports account for 60% of the country's income.

definition_en: to have a friendly relationship with someone

examples.text: I get along well with most of my colleagues.

definition_en: to remove or throw away something you do not want

examples.text: I must get rid of all these old magazines.

definition_en: to happen, especially in a way that is not planned

examples.text: How did this awkward situation come about?

definition_en: to meet someone or find something by chance

examples.text: I came across some old photographs in the attic.

definition_en: to receive money or property when someone dies

examples.text: She came into a fortune when her uncle died.

definition_en: to offer help or information

examples.text: The police asked witnesses to come forward.

definition_en: to continue doing something; to happen

examples.text: Please go on with your presentation.

definition_en: to experience a difficult situation; to examine carefully

examples.text: We are going through a tough restructuring process.

definition_en: to examine or inspect something carefully

examples.text: Let's go over the contract details one more time.

definition_en: to match or suit something

examples.text: That tie doesn't go with your shirt.

definition_en: to tolerate or endure an unpleasant situation or person

examples.text: I can't put up with this noise any longer.

definition_en: to suggest an idea or candidate for consideration

examples.text: He put forward a new cost-cutting proposal.

definition_en: to save money or time for a specific purpose

examples.text: She puts aside $200 every month for retirement.

definition_en: to assemble or create something by gathering parts

examples.text: We need to put together a report for the board meeting.

definition_en: to look or behave like an older family member

examples.text: She really takes after her mother in both appearance and personality.

definition_en: to return something you have purchased; to retract a statement

examples.text: I had to take the shirt back because it was damaged.

definition_en: to start a new hobby, activity, or job

examples.text: He decided to take up photography as a hobby.

definition_en: to employ someone; to accept responsibilities

examples.text: The company is taking on fifty new staff members this quarter.

definition_en: to cause something to happen

examples.text: The merger brought about massive changes in the management structure.

definition_en: to cause a government or leader to lose power; to reduce price

examples.text: The scandal eventually brought down the government.

definition_en: to produce light, smell, heat, or sound

examples.text: The chemical reaction gives off a strong toxic gas.

definition_en: to distribute something; to stop working (exhaustion)

examples.text: They were giving out free promotional brochures at the entrance.

definition_en: to happen in a particular way; to attend an event

examples.text: The presentation turned out to be a massive success.

definition_en: to refuse someone permission to enter

examples.text: The restaurant was full, so they had to turn away several customers.

definition_en: to publicly request or require something

examples.text: The current economic crisis calls for prompt government intervention.

definition_en: to reduce the amount of something you use or eat

examples.text: We need to cut down on office expenses this fiscal year.

definition_en: to disconnect an electricity/water supply; to interrupt

examples.text: The phone company cut off our service because we forgot to pay the bill.

definition_en: to make an agreement or price fixed; to secure

examples.text: We managed to lock in a low interest rate for our corporate loan.

definition_en: to visit someone informally without arranging it in advance

examples.text: Feel free to drop in whenever you are in the neighborhood.

definition_en: to remain outside home or a place, especially late at night

examples.text: My parents won't let me stay out past midnight.

definition_en: to become extremely tired or sick from working too hard

examples.text: If you keep working 80 hours a week, you will eventually burn out.

definition_en: to make something completely full

examples.text: I stopped at the gas station to fill up the tank.

definition_en: to solve a misunderstanding; (of weather) to become bright

examples.text: We held an urgent meeting to clear up the confusion regarding the project deadline.

definition_en: to write something down so that you do not forget it

examples.text: Let me note down your contact details before you leave.

definition_en: to become gradually less strong, loud, or noticeable

examples.text: We waited for the storm to die down before driving home.

definition_en: to connect to a computer system or network

examples.text: You need your corporate badge ID to log on to the intranet.

definition_en: to put on clothing to see if it fits and looks nice

examples.text: Never buy shoes without trying them on first.

definition_en: to go to an airport or station to say goodbye to someone

examples.text: We went to the main airport terminal to see her off.

definition_en: to start a journey; to cause a device to start operating

examples.text: We need to set off early tomorrow morning to avoid traffic jams.

definition_en: to exceed an allotted time; to hit someone with a vehicle

examples.text: The executive committee meeting ran over by twenty minutes.

definition_en: to move a short distance away; to view a situation objectively

examples.text: We need to stand back and examine the long-term strategic implications.

definition_en: to put things into a box or cupboard after using them

examples.text: It's time to pack away the holiday decorations until next year.

definition_en: to spend time in a place waiting or doing nothing

examples.text: I spent the entire afternoon hanging around the lobby waiting for him.

definition_en: to keep changing your physical position or location

examples.text: He is so restless, he constantly moves about the office during discussions.

definition_en: to come together to form a group around a central figure or object

examples.text: The entire engineering team gathered around the senior architect's monitor.

definition_en: to complete an action or plan that has been started

examples.text: It is vital to follow through on your commitments to build trust with clients.

definition_en: to survive a dangerous situation or recover from a severe illness

examples.text: Despite the economic downturn, our division managed to pull through successfully.

definition_en: to arrive or appear; to improve or develop

examples.text: When the right career opportunity comes along, you must seize it immediately.

definition_en: to register for an event, subscription, or service

examples.text: Hundreds of users have signed up for our beta enterprise platform.

definition_en: to support one person or group in an argument

examples.text: During the departmental dispute, she decided to side with the operations manager.

definition_en: to make a final decision between alternatives

examples.text: After lengthy internal debates, we settled on the cloud infrastructure plan.

definition_en: to make something more noticeable or distinct; to launch

examples.text: Our marketing campaign aims to bring out the core unique strengths of our product.

definition_en: to speak suddenly and without thinking about the consequences

examples.text: He accidentally blurted out the confidential merger details during lunch.

definition_en: to summarize or conclude a meeting or discussion

examples.text: Let's wrap up this alignment session and proceed to action items.

definition_en: to stop listening or paying attention to someone or something

examples.text: The presentation was so repetitive that half the audience tuned out.

definition_en: to develop a deep interpersonal connection with someone

examples.text: Annual corporate retreats provide excellent opportunities to bond with your peers.

definition_en: to begin to like someone or an idea after initial reluctance

examples.text: Initially skeptical, the board eventually warmed up to the innovative expansion strategy.

definition_en: to support and remain loyal to someone in times of trouble

examples.text: We will stand by our original vendor despite the transient supply chain bottlenecks.

definition_en: to take care of and protect someone's interests

examples.text: A great engineering lead always looks out for junior developers on the team.

definition_en: to make a sudden hostile verbal or physical attack

examples.text: Due to extreme stress, the product owner lashed out at the QA lead during standup.

definition_en: to exclude someone from an activity, organization, or information

examples.text: Minority shareholders complained that they were being shut out of major policy discussions.

definition_en: to become gradually less intimate or friendly over time

examples.text: Although they co-founded the firm together, they drifted apart over differences in core ideology.

definition_en: to bring a romantic relationship or business alliance to an end

examples.text: The corporate conglomerate decided to break up its non-profitable joint venture.

definition_en: to search for and ultimately find someone or something after exhaustive effort

examples.text: We managed to track down the root cause of the memory leak after deep debugging.

definition_en: to resolve small problems or discrepancies through negotiations

examples.text: Legal teams are working hard to iron out the remaining minor contract discrepancies.

definition_en: to intervene in a difficult situation in order to help or resolve it

examples.text: When negotiations stalled, the regional director had to step in to mediate the conflict.

definition_en: to use something as a backup plan when original options fail

examples.text: If the new billing API fails, we can always fall back on our existing legacy payment gateway.

definition_en: to accept additional duties, burdens, or employment

examples.text: I decided not to take on any additional side projects this quarter.

definition_en: to resign from an important position or executive role

examples.text: The CEO decided to step down after successfully leading the company for over a decade.

definition_en: to submit official documents, reports, or a letter of resignation

examples.text: All department heads are required to hand in their quarterly performance evaluations today.

definition_en: to begin working extremely hard and with intensive focus

examples.text: With the launch milestone approaching, we need to knuckle down and finalize the codebase.

definition_en: to acquire all shares of a company to achieve total ownership

examples.text: The venture capital syndicate offered to buy out the original tech startup founders.

definition_en: to provide emergency financial support to prevent imminent bankruptcy

examples.text: The central bank intervened to bail out the failing commercial financial institutions.

definition_en: to increase the retail selling price of merchandise for higher profit

examples.text: Luxury retailers typically mark up their signature accessories by over 300 percent.

definition_en: to cancel an uncollectible debt or regard an investment as a total loss

examples.text: The corporate accounting division decided to write off the non-performing bad loans.

definition_en: to contribute money or assistance toward a collective goal or gift

examples.text: Everyone in our division chipped in $20 to purchase a farewell present for the manager.

definition_en: to spend a large amount of money reluctantly on something

examples.text: We had to fork out over five thousand dollars for unanticipated enterprise database licenses.

definition_en: to spend a significant amount of money on luxury or pleasurable items

examples.text: They decided to splash out on a luxury corporate retreat in the Swiss Alps.

definition_en: to spend less time, money, or materials than is truly necessary

examples.text: You should never skimp on cybersecurity infrastructure when processing vital payment data.

definition_en: to do comprehensive reading in order to acquire expertise on a topic

examples.text: The senior developer spent the entire weekend reading up on distributed consensus protocols.

definition_en: to refresh or improve existing knowledge or skills that have faded

examples.text: I need to brush up on my advanced SQL performance optimization techniques before the interview.

definition_en: to withdraw permanently from an educational institution or course

examples.text: He decided to drop out of graduate school to dedicate his energy to founding a technology startup.

definition_en: to memorize large amounts of academic material immediately prior to an exam

examples.text: The university engineering students stayed up all night cramming for their calculus finals.

definition_en: to delay or retard the planned progress of a project or schedule

examples.text: Customs inspection holds up international enterprise hardware shipments by at least three days.

definition_en: to manage to find temporary time for an appointment in a busy schedule

examples.text: The technical director is fully booked, but she can fit you in for ten minutes at 4 PM.

definition_en: to continue for an unnecessarily extended period of time

examples.text: The corporate contract alignment meetings dragged on for over five consecutive hours.

definition_en: to change the scheduled time of an event to an earlier date

examples.text: The board of directors agreed to bring forward the quarterly financial audit by two weeks.

definition_en: to transition from a state of sleep to consciousness

examples.text: I usually wake up at 6 AM to verify overnight deployment telemetry logs.

definition_en: to wear formal, formal business, or elegant ceremonial attire

examples.text: You do not need to dress up for our internal casual technical engineering synchums.

definition_en: to clean dinner plates, cookware, and cutlery after dining

examples.text: After finishing our group dinner, we took turns washing up the kitchen utensils.

definition_en: to arrange physical spaces or codebases neatly and systematically

examples.text: We spent Friday afternoon tidying up our repository by purging obsolete feature branches.

definition_en: to register upon arrival at a hotel or airport

examples.text: Make sure to check in at least two hours before your scheduled international flight.

definition_en: to make a temporary stop during a long flight

examples.text: We decided to stop over in Frankfurt for two days during our business trip to Asia.

definition_en: to steer a vehicle to the side of the road and halt

examples.text: The traffic patrol officer signalled for the delivery driver to pull over immediately.

definition_en: to increase acceleration or operational tempo

examples.text: We need to speed up our data ingestion pipeline to meet the real-time SLA metrics.

definition_en: to duplicate electronic digital files for disaster recovery

examples.text: Enterprise database administrators must back up transaction logs every four hours.

definition_en: to connect an electrical appliance to a main power supply

examples.text: Simply plug in the diagnostic hardware console to begin deep system inspection.

definition_en: to terminate a voice call by disconnecting the line

examples.text: He abruptly hung up on the customer service agent out of sheer frustration.

definition_en: to return a previous incoming telephone call

examples.text: I am currently in an executive meeting; can I ring you back in thirty minutes?

definition_en: to slice culinary ingredients into small distinct fragments

examples.text: Chop up the fresh vegetables before adding them to the traditional stew pot.

definition_en: to spill out of a cooking vessel due to intense boiling

examples.text: Do not leave the milk on the kitchen stove unattended or it will rapidly boil over.

definition_en: to suffer temporary total syncope or loss of consciousness

examples.text: Due to intense summer heat in the facility, one of the field technicians passed out.

definition_en: to regain waking consciousness after faint or anesthesia

examples.text: It required over forty minutes for the operative patient to come round from anesthesia.

definition_en: to prepare muscles through mild exercise before a match

examples.text: Elite athletes always warm up for thirty minutes prior to rigorous tournament events.

definition_en: to eliminate an opponent from a competitive tournament

examples.text: Our corporate soccer squad was knocked out in the quarter-finals of the tech league.

definition_en: to become progressively occluded by dense atmospheric mist

examples.text: The bright summer sky began to cloud over shortly before the torrential afternoon downpour.

definition_en: to rain with extreme density and torrential force

examples.text: We stayed inside the lobby because it was pouring down outside during lunch.
